content_of_post_to_comment: I’ve never understood why we place so much weight on what someone does for a living. A profession can tell me how a person pays their bills. It cannot tell me how kind they are, how much they’ve survived, what they dream about, how they treat people, or how they carry their heart through this world. And yet somehow, we use those answers to measure importance. Job title. Income. Status. As if money automatically equals value. Which makes me wonder whether some people are truly asking who someone is, or simply trying to calculate where to place them. Human beings are so much bigger than their job titles.
comment: Even if we stopped using job titles, we'd still reach for other compressed proxies—taste, accent, aesthetics—because we need shortcuts to navigate scale, not just status.
